data.description:
## Finding (S9 — verified)
A 14-param query signature is duplicated across three surfaces; the shared `TicketQuery` filter object already exists but is only partially adopted, so adding a filter means editing three signatures.

Citations: `src/rebar/__init__.py:872`, `src/rebar/_reads.py:65`, `src/rebar/_mcp_reads.py:68` (14 params each); `src/rebar/_engine_support/ticket_query.py` (`TicketQuery`, the partially-adopted shared object).

## Remediation
Funnel all three surfaces through `TicketQuery`: `_reads.list_tickets` takes a TicketQuery; public `__init__.list_tickets` keeps its kwargs (public API) but builds+delegates a TicketQuery; the MCP tool builds one from its args (a `from_mcp` classmethod centralizes plumbing). Adding a filter then touches one dataclass.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All three query surfaces construct/forward a single TicketQuery
- [ ] The 14-field shape lives once (the dataclass), not in three signatures
- [ ] Public `list_tickets` keyword API is unchanged (back-compat)
data.title: Adopt TicketQuery across the three duplicated query surfaces
